methods.description: Analysis of Levallois reduction methods characterized by the production of blanks with predetermined shapes using different methods of flaking. This method is used to understand the technological elements and variability in prehistoric lithic assemblages.

steps.description: Identify and collect lithic assemblages containing Levallois technology from various archaeological sites.
steps.searchable_summary: collect lithic assemblages with Levallois technology from archaeological sites

steps.description: Study the reduction patterns or chaîne opératoire to recognize the methods of flaking used in Levallois technology.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze reduction patterns to identify flaking methods

steps.description: Analyze the hierarchical use of core surfaces and the preparation of the Levallois surface and preferential striking platform.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze core surface use and striking platform preparation

steps.description: Validate the chronological range and technological variability of Levallois reduction methods using Optically Stimulated Luminescence (OSL) dating.
steps.searchable_summary: validate Levallois reduction methods using OSL dating
